company_identity.name: FlowSignal
company_identity.value_proposition: Automated lead enrichment, scoring, and CRM delivery for B2B teams drowning in manual lead ops — set up in days, not quarters.
company_identity.tone: direct, warm, no corporate filler, no exclamation marks

icp_description: FlowSignal sells to B2B companies (Series A–B or bootstrapped-profitable, 20–200 employees) that have real inbound lead volume but no dedicated RevOps function. The best-fit buyer is visibly straining under manual lead handling — hiring their first ops/SDR roles, recently funded, or growing headcount fast — and already uses a CRM or basic tooling, so they understand the category. Companies over 1000 employees are out of scope: they build in-house or buy enterprise.

scoring_dimensions.ideal: B2B; Series A–B or bootstrapped-profitable; 20–200 employees
scoring_dimensions.notes: The 'can they actually buy' gate. Misfit here poisons downstream signals. ≤200 is a deliberate SMB/lower-mid scope, not a soft preference.

scoring_dimensions.ideal: Hiring SDR/ops/RevOps roles; 'drowning in leads' or manual-process language; recent funding (scaling pain); fast headcount growth
scoring_dimensions.notes: Top differentiator AMONG fitting companies. Equal weight to company_fit is a v1 hypothesis — revisit after #30 human spot-check.

scoring_dimensions.ideal: Inbound message states a concrete problem and/or timeline; specificity beats vague curiosity
Already runs a CRM or marketing/sales tooling — shorter sales cycle, understands the category
scoring_dimensions.notes: Fed directly by enrichment tech_stack detection.

scoring_dimensions.ideal: Funding round <6 months old or a recent product launch
scoring_dimensions.notes: News-sourced; low weight because news is the weakest-evidence enrichment component (collision noise).

hard_disqualifiers: personal email with no discoverable company
